summary: Iteration 96 - Apple/Google Pay mock wallet integration in DonationCheckout modal verified end-to-end on /giving (GivingDashboard). All UI flows passed: wallet slot renders, Google Pay button (Chromium), mock badge text, wallet sheet contents (Solomon AI, fund label, $25 amount, ••••4242 Visa, Face ID/screen-lock animation), cancel-without-success, auto-success transition to 'Thank You for Your Generosity!' screen with sp_txn_* reference, and zero console errors. CRITICAL FINDING: the wallet button is integrated into DonationCheckout (mounted in /giving GivingDashboard), but the member-facing /portal/give page (PortalGive.jsx) does NOT use DonationCheckout — it has its own custom give form, so members visiting /portal/give as instructed in the task will NOT see the Apple/Google Pay button. Verified with admin@solomonai.us via /giving where DonationCheckout actually renders.

integration_issues.issue: PortalGive.jsx does NOT mount DonationCheckout, so the new StripePaymentRequestButton wallet UI is NOT visible to members on /portal/give. The wallet integration only appears at /giving (GivingDashboard via give-card link). Members are redirected from /giving to /portal so they cannot reach the wallet button at all.

action_items: HIGH: Wire StripePaymentRequestButton (or DonationCheckout) into the member-facing /portal/give (PortalGive.jsx) so members actually see Apple/Google Pay. Currently only admins reaching /giving see the new button.
Confirm intended UX: should /portal/give open DonationCheckout modal, or should the wallet button be inlined directly into PortalGive's existing form above 'Give with Solomon Pay'?
critical_code_review_comments: StripePaymentRequestButton.jsx: clean separation of MOCK vs LIVE paths; live path is documented but not yet active. Wallet detection via UA is acceptable for mock; for LIVE, use stripe.paymentRequest().canMakePayment() before showing the button.

DonationCheckout.handleWalletSuccess correctly reuses handleSolomonPaySuccess so persistence is identical to card path. Backend /api/solomonpay/process accepted the mock token and returned a transaction_id.
Mock auth animation (~1.6s authing + ~0.75s success) feels realistic; total ~2.5s matches spec.
Sheet uses fixed inset-0 z-[100] which correctly overlays the slide-panel-overlay (z lower).
Gap: PortalGive.jsx (the route the task names) was not updated. Either route /giving for members or embed the wallet component in PortalGive.

success_rate.backend: n/a (no backend tests run, mock token accepted)

test_credentials.admin: admin@solomonai.us / Demo2026! (used to reach /giving where DonationCheckout renders)
test_credentials.member: member@abundant.church / Demo2026! (cannot reach /giving — redirected to /portal; /portal/give does not show wallet button)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Wallet mock UI itself is fully functional in DonationCheckout (verified via /giving + give-card with admin login). Re-test once main agent integrates the wallet button into PortalGive.jsx (or routes members to /giving). Test selectors that worked: data-testid='give-card' to open modal, donation-amount-25 to set amount, donation-wallet-slot wraps the button, google-pay-button (Chromium), wallet-mock-badge, wallet-mock-sheet, wallet-mock-cancel, donation-success-close. /api/solomonpay/process accepts the mock_tok_google_pay_* token successfully (returned sp_txn_f95d4bbc6aeb).
rca of the issue: Task spec referenced /portal/give but the implementation only mounted StripePaymentRequestButton inside DonationCheckout, which is only used by GivingDashboard (/giving). PortalGive.jsx is a completely separate component with its own form. Either the task description was incorrect or the integration was placed in the wrong screen. Fix: Either (a) import & render StripePaymentRequestButton inside PortalGive.jsx above the 'Give with Solomon Pay' button, or (b) replace PortalGive's custom form with a button that opens DonationCheckout.
